rationale: The cited paper is used repeatedly for core conceptual setup, not just background: Steenburgh explicitly classifies it as a unit-based decomposition approach and contrasts it with share- and decision-based approaches. He also relies on Van Heerde et al.'s interpretation of Gupta, uses their coffee example for a worked illustration, and cites their special-case result when deriving simplifying relationships. These uses inform the paper's theory development and derivations throughout the main argument.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Van Heerde et al. (2003)'s unit-based decomposition perspective and interpretive correction about switching versus expansion, then develops a broader framework that links unit-, share-, and decision-based decompositions. It extends that line by deriving formal unifying relationships and demonstrating them with the Van Heerde et al. coffee example.
